name: Panama Canal
summary: The Panama Canal is a giant water shortcut that lets huge ships sail from the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific Ocean. Special locks act like water elevators, lifting ships up over the jungly hills of Panama.
funFacts: Without the canal, ships had to sail right around South America. The shortcut saves them about 13,000 kilometres and many days at sea.
The canal opened in 1914 after more than 30 years of digging through jungle, swamps and mountains. Tens of thousands of people worked to build it.
Ships are lifted 26 metres up — as tall as an 8-storey building — using only gravity and water pouring down from a giant lake. No pumps needed!
Gatun Lake, the huge lake your ship sails across, was made by people in 1913 by damming a river. It used to be the biggest human-made lake on Earth.
In 2016 enormous new locks called Agua Clara and Cocoli opened. Their gates slide sideways like sliding doors instead of swinging open like normal doors.
thingsToDo: Watch your ship enter the Miraflores or Agua Clara Locks and rise up as millions of litres of water pour in.
Sail across Gatun Lake and grab binoculars to spot howler monkeys, sloths and toucans on the jungle islands.
Squeeze through the narrow Culebra Cut, sliced straight through a mountain, and pass under the tall Centennial Bridge.
Visit the Miraflores Visitor Centre to see ships up close and learn how the giant lock gates open and close.
Listen for ship horns and look for the little train engines, called mules, that guide huge ships through the locks.
Spot the Bridge of the Americas lit up at night as your ship reaches the Pacific Ocean side of the canal.

wildlife.name: Geoffroy's Tamarin
wildlife.description: Tiny black-and-white monkey with a fluffy white face that lives in family groups in the rainforest.

wildlife.name: Three-toed Sloth
wildlife.description: Slow furry animal that hangs upside down in trees and sleeps for most of the day.

wildlife.name: Harpy Eagle
wildlife.description: Panama's huge national bird with massive claws that can snatch monkeys right out of the trees.

wildlife.name: Blue Morpho Butterfly
wildlife.description: Big rainforest butterfly with wings that flash brilliant electric blue when it flutters in the sunlight.

localWord.meaning: Hello in Spanish, the language of Panama.

images.alt: A huge container ship squeezes through the narrow Miraflores Locks on the Panama Canal.
Little train engines called mules pull a big ship through the Gatun Locks.
A giant NeoPanamax ship rises inside the brand-new Agua Clara Locks built in 2016.
The tall white Centennial Bridge soars over the canal beside jungle-covered Gold Hill.
The arched steel Bridge of the Americas crosses the Pacific entrance of the Panama Canal.
The canal winds through steep jungle hills at the famous Culebra Cut near the Chagres River.
Calm waters of Gatun Lake, the giant human-made lake at the heart of the Panama Canal.
A small Geoffroy's tamarin monkey perched on a branch in the Panama rainforest.
A three-toed sloth hangs from a tree branch in Panama.
A harpy eagle, Panama's huge national bird, photographed in Soberania National Park.
A blue morpho butterfly with its wings open, showing brilliant electric-blue colour.
